2026-card.title: Parliamentary Election Voksmonitor
2026-card.description: The goal of Voksmonitor is to help voters engage with politics not only through party communications, but to learn about the positions of various political actors (in this case, factions and representatives) on a wide range of urban policy and professional issues. The app matches respondents with the faction or representative whose position is closest to their values based on their answers.

bp-card.title: Budapest General Assembly Voksmonitor
bp-card.description: The goal of Voksmonitor is to help voters engage with politics not only through party communications, but to learn about the positions of various political actors (in this case, factions and representatives) on a wide range of urban policy and professional issues. The app matches respondents with the faction or representative whose position is closest to their values based on their answers.

archive-section.description: K-Monitor has been running Voksmonitor since 2014, producing its Hungarian and English-language election calculator for parliamentary, European Parliament, and municipal (as well as mayoral) elections. You can find previous Voksmonitors in the archive.

methodology-section.title: How is Voksmonitor made?
methodology-section.description: Voksmonitor is a joint project of the K-Monitor and KohoVolit.eu nonprofit organizations, helping people learn how the factions and representatives of the Budapest General Assembly relate to the city's affairs.
methodology-section.step1-text: The questionnaire contains 28 General Assembly issues that were debated and decided upon between October 4, 2024 and May 28, 2025.
methodology-section.step2-text: The factions' answers were determined based on General Assembly votes and debates.
methodology-section.step3-text: These results are compared with your answers.
methodology-section.step4-text: Voksmonitor then shows which faction's or representative's opinion is closest to your values.
methodology-section.footer-text: The goal of Voksmonitor is to inform and support voter deliberation. The final decision is yours to make in the voting booth.

footer.tagline: Voksmonitor compares your opinions with the parties' platforms.

rolunk.support-text: Do you like Voksmonitor? Please support its creation!
rolunk.about-kmonitor:
At K-Monitor, we believe that corruption is a problem that affects every part of society and harms everyone – regardless of worldview, origin, gender, or political conviction. Corruption not only causes enormous economic damage, but also undermines trust in institutions and the rule of law. It threatens democracies and particularly severely affects regions where good governance is essential to eliminating hunger, poverty, and violence.
The best antidote to corruption is a society where citizens feel responsible for institutions and actively shape their own environment.
As a nonprofit organization, K-Monitor supports institutions, journalists, and individuals in the fight against corruption through community building, technological development, advocacy, and research.
The principles of our operation are transparency, independence, and critical thinking.
rolunk.about-kmonitor-links: If you are interested in learning more about our activities, visit our <website>website</website> or <blog>blog</blog>!

voksmonitorrol.heading: About Voksmonitor
voksmonitorrol.what:
Voksmonitor is an online application that compares your opinion with the positions of the factions and representatives working in the Budapest General Assembly. You can read more about the history of Hungarian (e.g. Vokskabin) and international (e.g. Stemwijzer, Wahl-O-Mat, Wahlrechner) election calculators similar to Voksmonitor and successful examples from around the world on Wikipedia.
Its goal is to help voters engage with politics not only through communications and campaigns, but to learn how decision-makers relate to urban policy issues.
The app matches respondents with the faction or representative whose position is closest to their values based on their answers.
voksmonitorrol.who:
Voksmonitor is jointly developed and operated by the K-Monitor and KohoVolit.eu nonprofit organizations.
The current edition selects 28 issues from the period between the inaugural session of the Budapest General Assembly on October 4, 2024 and May 28, 2025, in which representatives debated, deliberated, and made decisions.
The questions were compiled by K-Monitor staff to represent various areas of the General Assembly's decision-making – covering transport, housing, green space management, transparency, and urban development.
The representatives' answers were determined based on General Assembly votes.
voksmonitorrol.how:
The Voksmonitor questionnaire presents 28 General Assembly issues, each through supporting and opposing statements, to help respondents form their own position.
Respondents can indicate for each statement whether they agree with it, and how important they consider the given topic.
The app then compares the answers with the positions of factions and representatives, and calculates who shows the greatest alignment. The result is displayed as a percentage, indicating which political actor (faction or representative) is closest to the respondent's values.

modszertan.heading: Voksmonitor methodology
modszertan.p1: Voksmonitor is a joint project of <kmonitor>K-Monitor</kmonitor> and <kohopolit>KohoVolit.eu</kohopolit>, aimed at helping voters engage with politics not only through the communications of factions and representatives, but to learn their positions on urban policy and professional issues of the Budapest General Assembly.
modszertan.support-text: Do you like Voksmonitor? Please support its creation!
modszertan.body:
The application compares the user's answers with the positions of the factions and representatives working in the General Assembly, then shows who thinks most similarly to the respondent.
Voksmonitor previously worked by sending statements to party candidates, who had the opportunity to answer and justify them. If a party did not respond, their answers were determined based on their public positions. The current Voksmonitor breaks with this tradition: the positions of individual factions and representatives were classified solely based on the minutes and resolutions of the Budapest General Assembly. In return, we did not examine the agendas and debates of specialist committees, nor press conferences and political statements related to the General Assembly.
Voksmonitor helps respondents navigate 28 urban policy topics. The questions cover issues discussed by the General Assembly between October 4, 2024 and May 28, 2025. Of these, 3 topics cover Transparency, 3 Environmental Protection and Sustainability, 6 Transport and Mobility, 1 Community and Culture, 5 Social and Housing Affairs, 1 Social Issues and Equal Opportunities, 4 Urban Development, and 5 City Operations.
The creation of Voksmonitor was preceded by thorough research. We first browsed press materials and General Assembly minutes to find topics that generated lively debate, or that well illustrate the functioning and decision-making logic of the General Assembly, or which we considered important for the city.
Of course, the General Assembly dealt with many more than these 28 issues, and many topics were left out that were too technical, difficult to interpret, or not clearly articulable as questions. Our goal was to select issues that are understandable, relevant, and truly affect the life of Budapest.
On average, 35–50% of proposals come from the city leadership, either as official preparations or as proposals by the chief notary or the mayor. In contrast, the proportion of such proposals in Voksmonitor is below 20%, and the vast majority of the topics covered are representative initiatives. The main reason for this disproportion is that city leadership proposals are typically decisions related to daily operations or the implementation of ongoing projects, personnel decisions, or particularly complex regulations – for example, the adoption of urban planning legislation – which would have been difficult to summarize within the framework of a public policy statement.
In this sense, the methodological decision that Voksmonitor should primarily cover new initiatives favored politically "amplified" but substantively often simpler, single-focus representative motions, as opposed to the current agenda items of long-term processes.
After selecting the topics, we matched them with the related votes. The results of roll-call votes were extracted from the Budapest General Assembly minutes using our own software code and arranged into tables, then assigned to the selected urban policy issues. Given that we ultimately present the result of a specific vote, we often had to narrow the originally broadly formulated public policy topic to a sub-area so that the voting result remained representative of the statement. Moreover, there may be topics in Voksmonitor that came up multiple times during the past year in the Budapest General Assembly, sometimes with different voting results – in these cases, we ultimately had to choose a specific vote, which does not necessarily reflect the political positions evolving over the year, but in such cases we tried to select the most comprehensive vote.
Each question is accompanied by a brief explanation summarizing the main positions in the debate. Respondents can anonymously indicate their opinion with "agree", "disagree" or "skip" responses, and can also mark individual questions as important. The result is intended to guide orientation: it shows which factions and representatives are closest to the respondent's values.
Regarding the interpretation of voting results, it is also important to note that General Assembly votes are often preceded by debates in specialist committees, and certain amendments may already fail there or be integrated into the committee's consolidated amendment. In the General Assembly debate, it may therefore happen that we see an already compromised proposal, which the majority of factions more easily support. Furthermore, the most contentious issues may be voted down in the form of separate amendments or by requesting separate votes on individual resolution points, and in some cases they may not even make it onto the agenda. This is why, while there may be significant debate about details, the voting result often shows near-complete agreement among factions, or only 1-2 factions abstaining if an element particularly important to them was ultimately not included in the package. It is therefore worth keeping in mind when evaluating voting results that the proposal potentially already functions as the greatest common denominator among the supporting factions.

introduction.para1: The goal of Voksmonitor is to help voters engage with politics not only through party communications, but to learn about the positions of various political actors (in this case, factions and representatives) on a wide range of urban policy and professional issues. The app matches respondents with the faction or representative whose position is closest to their values based on their answers.
introduction.para2: Voksmonitor selects 28 issues from the period between the inaugural session of the Budapest General Assembly on October 4, 2024 and May 28, 2025, in which representatives debated, deliberated, and made decisions. The questions were compiled by K-Monitor staff to cover a wide range of decision-making areas before the General Assembly. Our goal was for the questions to reflect meaningful and understandable dilemmas affecting the city's functioning — from transport and housing to green space management, transparency, and urban development.
introduction.para3: Voksmonitor is a joint project of <kmonitor>K-Monitor</kmonitor> and <kohopolit>KohoVolit.eu</kohopolit>.

guide.agree-description: If a response matches the position of a faction or representative, it is worth one point.
guide.disagree-description: If the response is the opposite, one point is deducted.
guide.skip-description: If you have no position on the topic or do not wish to answer, you can proceed with the arrow on the right. The question will not be counted in the results.
guide.important-description: Topics important to the respondent can be marked with a star. These are counted with double weight by the program.
